summary: Strong Playing>UJB & The Last Time amid uneven first set
blurb: Set 2 shines with a tight Playing In The Band → Uncle John's Band sequence, The Last Time and a powerful Eyes Of The World jam. Jerry’s guitar is spotty early on but recovers for the second‑set highlights. Phil anchors the rhythm section while drums stay solid throughout.
review: The February 20, 1995 Delta Center show is a mixed bag that rewards listeners who can overlook some of Jerry Garcia’s off‑kilter moments. The first set stumbles through a shaky Bertha and an uninspired El Paso, but the second set delivers the real meat: a soaring Playing In The Band that segues into an inspired Uncle John's Band jam, followed by a fiery Eyes Of The World that showcases the band’s trademark percussion interplay. The highlight reel continues with The Last Time and a heartfelt Rain encore, both of which capture Phil Lesh’s steady bass work and the tight drumming of Mickey Hart and Bill Kreutzmann. Vince Welnick’s keyboards are clean‑tuned on the stronger cuts, though they never fully lift the show out of its average‑mid‑90s plateau.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Both recordings describe a mixed performance: positive notes on Playing > UJB, The Last Time, Little Red Rooster and strong percussion, but consistent criticism of Jerry’s uneven guitar work and several weak songs (Bertha, El Paso). The balance tips slightly positive, yielding a modest +0.3 sentiment boost.

best_recording.reason: Clean soundboard with good instrument separation; captures the standout second‑set moments while remaining free of major dropouts.

key_highlights: Playing In The Band → Uncle John's Band sequence is a show‑defining peak.
The Last Time and Rain deliver emotional closure despite earlier unevenness.
Strong percussion on Eyes Of The World showcases Mickey and Billy’s chemistry.
Vince Welnick’s keyboard tone is clean on the better cuts, adding clarity.

band_performance.Jerry: Inconsistent throughout – solid tone early on, but many moments of sloppy timing and off‑kilter phrasing in jams like Cassidy and El Paso.
band_performance.Phil: Provides a steady bass foundation; highlighted on Rain and generally the strongest core performance of the night.
band_performance.Bob: Mix often too loud; occasional lapses in leadership during jams without notable positive highlights.
band_performance.Vince: Keyboard work is clean‑tuned on strong songs, though overall contributions are seen as underwhelming compared to the band’s energy.
band_performance.Mickey: Drums are praised for balance and drive, especially during the post‑jam sections.
band_performance.Billy: Percussion is powerful and well‑balanced; shines on Eyes Of The World and throughout the second set.
